name: hindi_smoke
description: 10 Hindi test utterances for Sprint 1 baseline sanity check. Short, diverse, covers core categories. The full 300-utterance golden set is composed in Sprint 2.

utterances.text: आप कहाँ से आ रहे हैं?
utterances.transliteration: āp kahāṁ se ā rahe haiṁ?

utterances.text: वाह! कितनी सुंदर है यह शाम!
utterances.transliteration: vāh! kitnī sundar hai yah śām!
utterances.english_gloss: Wow! How beautiful this evening is!

utterances.text: विश्वविद्यालय में पढ़ाई करते समय मुझे अपने सहपाठियों के साथ बहुत सी बातें सीखने को मिलीं।
utterances.transliteration: viśvavidyālay meṁ paṛhāī karte samay mujhe apne sahapāṭhiyoṁ ke sāth bahut sī bāteṁ sīkhne ko milīṁ.
utterances.english_gloss: While studying at the university, I got to learn many things together with my classmates.
utterances.notes: Sanskritic compound 'viśvavidyālay' (viśva+vidyā+ālay), compound verb 'sīkhne ko milīṁ', temporal postposition phrase 'karte samay', and the plural oblique 'sahapāṭhiyoṁ ke sāth' — stresses long-form morphology and postposition stacking.

utterances.text: हमारे CEO ने इस quarter में एक बढ़िया presentation दिया।
utterances.transliteration: hamāre CEO ne is quarter meṁ ek baṛhiyā presentation diyā.
utterances.english_gloss: Our CEO gave a great presentation this quarter.
utterances.notes: English terms (CEO, quarter, presentation) embedded mid-sentence — tests Latin-script code-switching within Devanagari.

utterances.text: दिल्ली में २६ जनवरी, 2026 को एक बड़ा कार्यक्रम हो रहा है।
utterances.transliteration: dillī meṁ 26 janavarī, 2026 ko ek baṛā kāryakram ho rahā hai.
utterances.english_gloss: A big event is happening in Delhi on 26 January, 2026.
utterances.notes: Mixes Devanagari digits (२६) with Arabic digits (2026), plus named entity (Delhi) and a date expression.

utterances.text: आदरणीय अतिथियों का हम हार्दिक स्वागत करते हैं।
utterances.transliteration: ādaraṇīya atithiyoṁ kā ham hārdik svāgat karte haiṁ.
utterances.english_gloss: We extend a heartfelt welcome to our honourable guests.
Dude, shall we go for a movie tomorrow?

utterances.text: क्षत्रिय ऋषि के यज्ञ में त्रिकोण और ज्ञान की चर्चा हुई।
utterances.transliteration: kṣatriya r̥ṣi ke yajña meṁ trikoṇ aur jñān kī carcā huī.
utterances.english_gloss: At the Kshatriya sage's yajna, there was a discussion of the triangle and of knowledge.
utterances.notes: Dense conjuncts and retroflexes: क्ष (kṣ), त्र (tr), ज्ञ (jñ), ऋ (r̥), ण (ṇ), ष (ṣ), ञ — stresses the Sanskritic phoneme inventory.

utterances.text: मेरे दादाजी हर शाम बरामदे में बैठकर अपने बचपन की कहानियाँ सुनाते हैं, और हम सब मिलकर बड़े चाव से उनकी बातें सुनते हैं।
utterances.transliteration: mere dādājī har śām barāmde meṁ baiṭhkar apne bacpan kī kahāniyāṁ sunāte haiṁ, aur ham sab milkar baṛe cāv se unkī bāteṁ sunte haiṁ.
utterances.english_gloss: Every evening my grandfather sits on the verandah and tells stories from his childhood, and all of us together listen to him eagerly.
utterances.notes: ~32 syllables across two clauses joined by 'aur' — tests prosody, breath control, and long-form coherence.
